The first thing we'll talk about is how to create your free plus shipping offer. And remember yesterday I talked about there's this very specific way you want to make sure you create this. If you create it wrong, it's not going to be nearly as effective as if you create it the right way. Okay, so here it is, a free plus shipping offer. First thing you want to know is you want to make sure that the product that they are ordering, the free CD you would say in this example, it should be at least 30 to 60 minutes long. Okay, I've only seen one person ever pull it off with a CD that was like 20 minutes long. Okay, every single other person that's going to try to go lower and that kind of blown up in their face because people get it and they don't feel like they're getting anything. Okay, you have to be able to make sure that they feel like when they get this product that they paid free plus shipping for, that they've got something of value in their hands. It needs to be at least 30 to 60 minutes long to be able to establish that value. Okay, there's two ways to do it. The first way, which is what I recommend all you guys do probably is to do an interview. Okay, and it can be either you interviewing somebody, somebody interviewing you. It doesn't really matter, but the easiest way for most people is to do an interview. Now, especially if you haven't done these a lot and what's going to happen, first of all, get on a call by yourself. You're going to be super nervous. And if you try to talk for 60 minutes, it becomes very, very difficult. So make sure that, for most of us that do an interview, find a friend, find a spouse, find somebody, give them a list of questions, have them interview, have them interview you, interview you and all that kind of stuff. It's going to make it so that you sound better on the CD and you're more excited to listen to. The other thing is when you're on the call, especially if you do this solo, make sure that you don't talk at your normal tone. Okay, because I'm on the phone. This is actually the tone that I normally talk at. I just talk like this for an entire hour with you guys. You probably get really tired and get really bored. So what you have to do is you have to add a little energy and excitement because that's what keeps people listening to your thing. I mean, you have to picture somebody in their car listening to you talk. And if your voice is really monotone and slow and boring, it's going to be really hard for them to listen to. But if you're exciting and upbeat and people can hear the passion in your voice, it makes them a lot better to listen to. So I want to make sure that, especially if you're doing solo, but interview as well, makes you have a lot of energy, your voice is high, you know it's my fluctuations in my voice, that type of thing. And again, for most of you, I'd recommend doing an interview unless you've done this kind of thing a lot in the past. Okay, next thing is that during this free plus shipping offer, you're going to be teaching your core secrets. And last we'll be talking about, we sat down and we found here are my seven secrets, my 10 secrets, my 12 secrets. On the CD, you're going to be actually teaching the core secrets. But the key here, and this is very, very vital, make sure you take notes on this. You do not teach anybody how to do something on your free plus shipping offer. What you are doing, you're teaching somebody why they do something. Here's a big difference. You do not want to teach somebody how to do something, you want to teach somebody why to do something. Now, the reason why I say that is because the teaching of the how, that happens in the microcontinuity element of this. I'll talk about that when I get to that portion. But on your CD, you're teaching the why. So this is why we do this. This is why you want to go to Google and do something. And this is why you want to set up an account and do this. This is why you want to lose weight. But you don't take down the how. You say you want to eat good, you want to make sure you eat grains. You talk about the why you want to do it. But again, you do not want to get into the how. Hopefully that makes sense for you guys. There is a distinction, but think about that. Because if you get somebody the how on the CD, then there's no reason for that person to keep learning from you. And the other thing is it's hard to teach the how on the CD. You want to teach the why. So module number one is how to overcome being overweight by eating carbohydrates. And the reason why you want to eat carbohydrates is carbohydrates are better for your body. And again, explaining why, why, why, why, why. But I'm not saying how. I'm saying what you want to do is you want to go to the grocery store. You want to go to the noodle aisle. You want to make sure when you're in the noodle aisle, you're buying this, this, and this. That's the how. I don't want to get into the how. I don't want to get into the how. That's what I'm going to be teaching during my micro-con, nudie element, not so much during the front end free plus shipping offer. Does that make sense for everybody? Hopefully. Now the next thing, this is very important, is when you're on the CD, you want to always say after each of your secrets you want to explain. So here's why you want to eat carbohydrates. Now what I want to always mention is I want to say now, I don't have time on the CD right now to explain to you exactly how you want to eat carbohydrates. I'm going to be teaching you that in the weekly email training. So I share the secret. I teach why the secret. But then I say for the how, I say I'm going to teach you exactly how to do it in the weekly email trainings. So keep kind of referencing it. And so the phrase I like using is I wish I had time right now. If you guys ever hear me speak on stage, you'll notice I do a lot of this. I'm speaking on stage at a seminar or a workshop. I teach a lot about why you do some things. This is kind of the way that it works. And then I always say I wish I had more time so I could go into exactly how this works. But I'm just, you know, I want 90 minutes. I keep going. I keep moving through this content. The reason why I do this is because people, they get this kind of feeling like, no, I wish he had time too. So I want to know how. And what's nice is at the end when you present your product or the offer whatever you're selling, what people can actually get the how to do it, that is where you get people to stand up and to purchase your product. It's the same thing within this situation. You're explaining why. Here's secret number one is this. I mean, you can go and eat carbohydrates and you make sure the carbohydrates you find are the best kind. You don't want this, you know, this kind, through this kind, you want to make sure you have this kind. I wish I had time right now on the CD to explain to you exactly how to find the right ones. But unfortunately, I don't. We only have 60 minutes. But what I'm going to do is next, you know, week number two of my, of the weekly email coaching, I'm going to actually show you how to do this. Okay. Now, secret number two is, and I go into secret number two. Okay. Secret number two, I share the why behind the secret. And again, I end with, I wish I had time to explain exactly how to find the stuff, but I don't. But what I'll do is actually in the weekly training that you're getting through email, I'll make sure I cover that as well. Okay. Do you guys see how that works? So again, during this whole content creation, on this whole front of the CD, I teach you why and then how I'm explaining them, I'm explaining how they can get through my microcontinuity training. Okay. Very, very important. One of the things, and I mentioned this last week as well, Jimmy D. Brown said something once, it was very, very powerful. He said, you want to make sure your content is useful but incomplete. Okay. So by listening to your free CDs, it's going to be very useful. So you get a very good understanding of why something works, kind of the ways it works, you know, understanding of the secrets, the big, kind of a broad overview of everything. So it's very useful, but it's incomplete. They still don't know how to do it. In the how, that's what comes in your weekly microcontinuity training. Okay. So useful but incomplete. Okay. Now, I want to break down the actual call. So if you do a 60-minute call, there's a timeline, a very important timeline you want to make sure you follow. Okay. First thing is you want to start out with some kind of introduction, whether it be you introducing yourself, whether you have a guest who's introducing you or you're introducing guests, some kind of introduction to get them reexcited. Because what's going to happen? A lot of you guys are going to buy CDs. They're going to get it. And they're going to put it in the box. They're going to get it. And they're going to put it in their car, their home. And they're going to be like, you know, what is this again? And you need to be able to make sure that during the introduction, you're reexciting them about what it is that they just purchased. Okay. So you just purchased this CD, which is the seven secrets to how to overcome, how to overcome, well, what's a niche? How to overcome eating, how to overcome overeating. Okay. And on these seven secrets, we're going to show you the exact step-by-step process you need to overcome eating forever. Okay. And I'm here with my special guest. His name is Mike Thompson. And he is one of the world's leading teachers on this. Or, you know, he bought a book one time and read it. And now he's regurgitating it. He's the guru for the call today. It can be whatever you want. It doesn't really matter. But make sure you're introducing it and building some kind of credibility for the person. Again, a person could be you or it could be somebody else. Make sure you're building some credibility for that person so that it kind of, again, reminds them of what they purchased, why they purchased it, and why they should be listening to you and why that should be exciting. Okay. Now, the next thing is that you've got a very short period of time to catch people's attention. Okay. People, everyone's got really short attention span. And so about the five-minute mark, what you need to do is you need to lay down your hook. We call it your set. This is about a five-minute mark. After you've done the introduction, before you get into the training of it, you want to say something very specific. You want to say, okay, now, before we get started, I want to let all of you guys know that at the end of this call, I want to tell you that something very, very important. I want to tell you that. And this is not something that anybody else can get access to. Just people who listen to this CD. Okay. So again, I'm going to show you this very end of this call after I've shared all seven of these secrets. But this is something I want to make sure you stand at the end of the call because it's that powerful. Okay. I just set a hook. Someone's got that hook and they said, wow, there's something powerful happening at the end of this call that I need to make sure I stay on for. Okay. Now, I want to make sure you guys understand that because, again, we got this back in the days of the rice. We used to have a lot of telecenters. We used to watch monitor people's drop off rates, how many people were on when they would drop off and that kind of stuff. And usually about the five to 10-minute mark, a lot of people, you've got 20 or 30% of people will start dropping off. Okay. Because about that point, they get on, they hear the introduction, they're like, yeah, this is not what I want. Or there's, you know, and so if you just go straight into introduction, straight to teaching, you're going to lose a whole bunch of people. Because that's about the, it's a 20 to 30% of people are dropping off about that point. And in your car, I'm sure it's even higher because they're putting a CD in the car, they're driving somewhere, you know, that kind of stuff. And so you've got to get something solid fast that hooks them, that makes them want to stick through the entire 60 minutes. And you want to play some kind of hook. And again, the way I do it is, hey, before I get to the training on this call, I want to tell you that I've got a very, very important secret. Or, you know, I got seven secrets from the story, but I actually figured out just recently an eighth secret. It's completely different than nobody else even knows about. In fact, I just barely got it on, learned it just in time to share on the CD, but I'm not going to be able to share it to the end. So make sure you stand for the entire thing so I can share it to you with the 13th secret or the 12th secret. However you want to set up, but make sure that before you get into the content, you're setting some kind of hook that's keeping that person around to the end. Okay, it's a very, very important aspect to this whole thing. The next thing is you want to start, after that point, you want to start teaching. You first off can share secret number one, spend five to ten minutes talking about the secret, having some of the, again, have somebody interview and ask you questions about it. You just respond back to it. And if you just sit down and talk about a secret for five minutes, ten minutes, it will take a little bit really hard, especially at first. But if you can sit there and just ask somebody, or have somebody ask you a question, then you just respond to it or share a story about it or something like that. It becomes very, very easy. That's why a lot of times it's easier to do this with an interview. Okay? And then again, at the end of each one of these little secrets, I wish I had time to show you exactly how to do it, but I don't. But the good news for you is that you are part of my microcontinuity. I won't say microcontinuity, but you're part of my weekly training program. You're getting these video emails from me, these audio emails from me, each week showing you the how that I know you guys really need to be successful with this. Okay? Then you do secret number two, and you repeat that through each of your secrets. You've got seven, you've got ten. You just repeat that throughout the rest of the process. Okay, and what I would do before you call is kind of set a timeline as well. Because if you've got 15 secrets, you've got to be able to talk a lot faster and keep your things a lot shorter, be able to cover all 15 secrets. I think it's important to share as many or to share your entire system up front on a CD. I like to share my whole system so that everyone who then goes and buys my other products, they go to my training, they have a good base understanding of the whole system that I'm teaching. Okay? That's why you guys notice if you watch my presentation to get you guys into this class, I pretty much taught the whole microcontinuity system. I want to make sure you guys had a good understanding of how to alert and the whys behind it so that now during this presentation we can dig into the house to do it. Okay? All right. Now, at about the 50-minute mark, so we've got 10 more minutes left on the call, about this point you should be done with all of your secrets, kind of going through, teaching all the secrets. And this point is where you want to say, look, at the beginning of the call, I promised you guys I was going to give you something really, really special. And I want to tell you what that is right now. Okay, now, and you want to make sure you want to kind of break away from the whole secret thing and say, you know, start so that they realize that there's something special happening. So say, look, before we go on, at the beginning of this call, I told you I was going to give you this really cool thing. Now, if you're sitting down, make sure, or if you're not, make sure you're sitting down and taking notes because this is the most powerful thing I want to share with you. Okay? Notice I did that. It kind of got, everyone got quiet. They kind of listened and their ears perk up and they're like, okay, what's this thing? And this is where you reveal your secret. Okay? The big thing, the big hook. It could be a secret. It could be a link to a special PDF they get for free. It could be anything you want. I don't really care what the hook is, but as long as there's something there that you can then give them at the end to really make them feel, you know, kind of feel good that they went through the whole process. Okay? And the last at the end is you do your close. Now, your close could be a couple of different things. If you're just doing a CD like this, you could just close and you'll make you so much more information. Go to .comsecrets.com and end. Now, some of you guys are going to be using this. You already have back-end products in place. Now, if I already had a back-end product in place, like a home study course or something else I was going to sell, about this point is where I would say, look, a lot of you guys have benefited from this. And why do you know that I have got some, you know, if you want more, I've got a weekly training as you're already part of it. I've also got something that a lot of you guys are going to want. It's my new home study course. It's whatever. At this point, I can go ahead and I can sell my other products. If you have any products right now, don't worry about it. You don't have to sell anything on a CD. Don't make that not keep you from creating this thing. Okay? In fact, three or four of my front-end products never sell anything on them. Yet they still work as really good as front-end products. So don't not create your CD until you have something to sell. You don't have to sell on this. You could just teach, go through this process like this. Now you've got, again, a solid product that's a perfect front-end for you guys. Okay? So it's pretty simple. Again, it's not that hard. There's just a very precise layout on how you do things. If you add these little elements here that I shared with you, these elements will keep people listening to your CDs. So what's interesting is we found out, the first time we ever did this, with our first free DVD offer, we found some really weird stats. And we had a call center that was calling all the people. It was in our own internal call center. You can call it that. We had two people who were on little phones pretending like we knew what we were doing. But they would order a CD and we'd call them on the phone to try to sell them. What we found was interesting is that if somebody did not listen to the CD or watch the DVD, it was hard to sell. But if somebody watched the DVD, it was pretty much like a lay-down sale. People would say, oh, yeah, that's really good. I want to be part of this. Because I think at the end of the DVD, we mentioned something about our coaching program and something like that. So we would call people. They said, yeah, I watched that. And I rise where I call them and I want to be part of it. So if someone actually listens to your CD and goes through it, for you to come back now and sell them stuff in the future is 1,000 times easier. So you want to make sure that you have this tool in their hand because it's going to make your job selling everything else you have 1,000 times easier in the future. Okay. Now we'll talk about the next part of this. And this is actually creating your micro content. This is the content they're going to be editing through email. I call it your micro content. Okay. Now again, the micro content does not need to be long. A lot of people, we first started explaining to people how to be an hour long video every single week. It does not have to be that. It's called micro continuity for reasons. You can keep it very short and concise, usually between 10 and 20 minutes, whether it be an audio or a video or let's say a PDF, usually five to six pages, will be enough content to keep them around week after week. Okay. So 10 to 20 minutes long is as long as the audio needs to be. Okay. Now this audio or this video or this PDF, the folks of this now should be teaching how to do the how. Okay. We talked before. You do not teach the how in a free CD. You do teach it in the weekly micro content. This is where you teach the how. Okay. Now, the other powerful thing about the micro content is something called, I'm going to mess up the pronunciation, but the Zergarna effect. And some of you may have heard of the Zergarna effect or may not have heard of the quote from Wikipedia. I actually learned this concept originally from Mark Joyner, but it says the Zergarna effect states that people remember uncompleted or interrupted tasks better than completed ones. Okay. If you read about Wikipedia, it's kind of cool. It's talking about people who go on a study and they take frequent study breaks and they go and do unrelated things like go and play basketball or run around the block or go eat something and come back. They actually retain information a lot longer than people who sit down and study for like five hours in a row at a time. It's really interesting. But the same thing happens in marketing and in sales and all this kind of stuff is if you have a thought that's uncompleted or uninterrupted, it's going to people can remember that much more. So you see this happening all the time in TV. Okay. How often does an hour before the news comes on, you see these little two-second news clicks saying, you know, man fell off a bridge and almost died. Find out what is the person driving the car said at 9 o'clock tonight or 10 o'clock tonight where it gives you a little piece of it. Okay. So proper is this well too. Reality shows this really well too. As soon as they get the commercial break, you know, and it may be especially, you know, sometimes I'd be kind of ending on kind of slow note, break for the commercial break, you know, up next. And they get back to see what's going to happen. And they give you some really big hook that keeps you coming back. Okay. My favorite show 24 does this perfectly. At the end of every single show, they have a thing that just hooks you on what in the world is going to happen next. Okay. And since our garlic effect is causing something. So bringing up a question in some of mine that cannot be completed is going to keep them around. So what you want to do at the end of each of these 10 to 20 minute things is if you complete the teaching, you complete the training, whatever it is. And then you say, hey, next week you're going to be getting something that's very, very powerful. It's one of the most important parts of this whole process. It's the whatever. And you kind of explain it. And during this process, I'm going to teach you this, this, and this. So make sure that you guys are here next week. Make sure you download the audio because next week we're going to talk about this. Okay. The Zergarovic effect is a thing you're using. I'm almost like a hook. You're opening a question. You're opening a thought into their head that they cannot close on their own. Okay. Again, with the news, they're saying, hey, news tonight at five o'clock some guy skydived and guess what happened? You cannot answer that on your own, no matter how hard you try. And you want to do the same thing. You want to, in your content, these 10 to 20 minute content is to, obviously, you're opening a door to the next lesson that they cannot close on their own. It's going to open a question that they cannot close on their own. That's how the Zergarovic effect works. Now, Mark Joyner, when he taught me originally, he was teaching me in how to, when you're writing a self-letter. And he was a master. If you watch the way he writes self-letters, is he would introduce a self-letter and he would bring up a concept. He would look every, I want to tell you about the most powerful thing I've ever learned about whatever. But before I do that, I want to talk about this. And he starts going on and starts talking about something. So he brought up this thing. He had this like uneasy feeling in your gut saying, wait, he just told me he's going to tell me this thing. What is it? And you keep waiting, and you keep waiting. And during that process, he's not delivering the content and stuff that he wants to get to you. And then after a certain period of time, he says, okay, now what I told you about was this. This is the answer to it. Okay. That's kind of how it works. So make sure that you're using the Zergarovic effect every single time during each of these 10 to 20-minute trainings. Talking about your next week, I'm going to talk about the next step of the process, how it works together with the earlier step. Okay, you guys know, I do this during these trainings as well. Last week, or last week at the end of the training, I gave you guys some hooks that we're going to talk about this week. I mean, the same thing at the end. This one I'm going to talk about what we're going to talk about next week. I'm going to get you guys motivated, excited, and open some questions you cannot answer on your own unless you're in the next training. Okay, again, Zergarovic effect is a very important part of this whole process. Again, we talked earlier, especially during the sales process of this course, we talked about how normal continuity programs, most people are only staying on three to four months on a good continuity program. Okay, and the reason why is because there's nothing really, usually some of the logs and membership side, they've got a whole bunch of stuff. What's keeping them around? What's the hook? There's nothing, but with a weekly spoon fed, spoon delivered continuity program that we're teaching and that we're doing, we are giving them peace and saying, look, next week you get the next step. Next week you get the next step and you keep breaking it up. Okay, so next question a lot of people said, well Russell, my course was seven steps. I don't want a seven week microcontinuity program. I want more in that. How should I do that? So that's where this slide came from. Okay, first somebody who's going to take your outline of your seven to 10 secrets you created last week. Okay? Hopefully you have that on Friday. You can get your seven to 10 secrets. The next step you want to do is you want to break out each of these secrets into two or three steps. Okay? So my secret is to drive traffic through pay per click. I don't want one weekly continuity training teaching pay per click. I want to break it down to, okay, week number one, how to do your research. Week number two, how to write an ad. Week number three, how to blah, blah, blah, and keep picking different things. Okay? So each of your secrets, you should be able to break down into steps. You look at any book. You look at a table of content, especially the big ones like the Idiot's Guide to whatever. You'll have a chapter which is whatever, and then there's like 10 or 15 subchapters kind of in there. Okay? So I promise you that every one of your secrets, you should be able to break down into at least two or three steps. And some of you guys are going to want to break down into five or 10. But at least two or three steps, break down each secret into at least two or three steps. So let's say for example, let's say you have 10 secrets. You've got three steps for each secret. Now it's now a 30-week microcontinuity program. Okay? That takes you over longer than six months. Okay? And if you can think of four steps, now it becomes 40 weeks. Okay? Five steps becomes a year-long continuity program. So again, it just comes down to how creative you can be, how many individual steps you can go through. And nice about steps too, I like, I love to get into the steps because ready was when I started the microcontinuity program, I said, okay, again, this is secret number one. The secret's got five steps. This is going to teach you step number one. This is the one you want to go out and implement today. And they go, you teach them about it, go implement it next week. I'm going to talk about step number two. Now step number two is going to teach you this. Okay? And again, you're using these are going to correct the poem. Step number two. Okay, and then at the end of that case, you've gone through three weeks. You should be done now with secret number one. Now focus on secret number two. Here's step one of secret number two. Here's step two of secret number two. Here's step three, and you start pulling them through that process. Okay? But again, for most of you guys, I promise, if you spend a little time and kind of diagram this out, you should be able to break your content into at least 20, 30, 40 weeks without too much effort. Okay? Now each of these steps becomes one week's worth of content. Pretty simple. And for each step, to kind of strengthen the content, a lot of you guys are going to create, you know, you're going to record 10 minute audio and send that out for some of them. It might not be quite enough because some people, I mean, even if you're not giving them a lot more content, people want to feel like they're getting more content. So you want to break out from these. You want to find different resources, tools, examples, or case studies that kind of go along with that. Okay? So let's just say, for this example, secret number one is driving traffic with pay per click. My first step is doing keyword research. So for that week, I'd say, okay, I spent 10 minutes saying, okay, the first key is finding out keyword research. How you get keyword research is first you go to this site. You type this, and then you go here, and you do this and that kind of stuff. So I'm going to talk about it for 10 minutes, 15 minutes, let's say, and then I say, okay, below I've got a bunch of resources to show you some ideas. So if you're teaching, let's say, pay per click, or you're teaching, whatever you're teaching, there's going to be tools and resources that people might need to make that process happen. And if they happen to be tools and resources that you recommend with your affiliate link, you happen to make money with them. How good of a problem is that for you? Okay? So make sure that each of these steps has some kind of tool or resource that will make it easier, that will make it better, and find your affiliate links for those tools. And you're going to get a lot of people who are going to be purchasing these products and services through your affiliate links because you've got them right there in the microcontinuity content. Okay? So resources and tools, this again is one of your big profit streams. Okay? Next thing is examples and case studies. I like to have some kind of example or case study whenever I teach. So I don't want to just say, hey, here's a step in one is do this. If I can say step in one is do this. And by the way, here's an example or a case study of somebody who did it and this is their results. If you can share a case study or a result, it now makes that concept a thousand times stronger in the person's mind. Okay? It's totally made of, they've volitified it and I don't know if that's the right word. It becomes valid in their mind saying, yes, this is true because so and so did it right over here. Okay? So you want to make sure again, get tools and resources for each of these steps with your affiliate links if possible. And the problem, I was very short-sighted apparently. At the end of the first time we printed, I think we sold four or five thousand. We printed all these CDs out. And at the end of it, I said, hey, February 22nd, whenever we're having this workshop here in Boise, I want to invite you guys to it. It worked really well up until February 22nd, and then all the CDs became useless to us. And so what I do, I mean, it's a good idea to be able to have something where you can change the front and the back, but make sure when you do this kind of stuff to kind of keep it so that it's evergreen, so that you don't have to go back and keep re-burning CDs. What I would do is say something like, hey, in the very near future we're doing it. We're doing a workshop. And if you go to this website here, you can find out dates and times of the next one. It's something like that where you're not tying in dates and times where it makes it so that your product's not evergreen, but you have the ability to push them to a website. I apologize for everyone who's gone to college like me. I spent five years there. In classes, they teach a lot about why and that kind of stuff. But I remember sitting there at graduation with my cap and gown on, and I'd spent five years going through school to get a degree in computer information systems. I remember looking around, the other people sit next to me. I remember telling the guy, I'm like, we're graduating today. I'm like, do you know how to program? The guy said, no. I said, neither do I. I was on school for five years. And he said, well, what they do, they kind of explain how it works. And then on the job is where you learn how it actually works. So I spent five years and all this money to learn the why, but I still have to go on the job to learn how. And that's the reality of how education usually works. You just have a good overview of the process before you can ever dig into the how. Now, obviously, the school can teach you some of the hows, but not enough that you can really go. I mean, college is the best example of useful but incomplete that I've ever really experienced myself as a student. And you don't really learn the how until you're on the job. I like doing a semi-force because I like giving people the ability not to get it if they don't want it. It's up to you. Again, that's kind of a personal preference. But you want to have at least a semi-force in there. Otherwise, you're going to lose the – a lot of people won't even know or they're going to confuse having two options and that kind of stuff. And so basically, the way you sell – we'll talk about copy when we get to the sales page, copy and how you position it the right way. But when you position it, you can really position it as a huge benefit to them. You know, and say, you got a CD, but I'm not going to leave you alone. I'm going to be walking through the process week by week, holding your hand, making sure that you're implementing this stuff because we don't want to see people who aren't implementing this, that type of thing. Okay. Charles, he says, Russell, how would you position your free offer in training if your objective is to refer them to good affiliate products on an ongoing basis? Oh, great question. In fact, we've got a couple of sites we're building right now based on the same model where we're having a friend that's talking about a topic. So let's say it's – let's say for example, PaperClick. Okay? This is a good example. So PaperClick. We have a free site talking about PaperClick, the weekly continuity program teaching case. Step number one is doing keyword research. And how many keyword tools are there out there? You know, and it's a great way to promote all the keyword tools. Week number two is, you know, now you need this. And then week number three is each week you're teaching the process, but you're promoting products really hard through the process. Now, one thing I would do, be very, very aware of is if you're doing that type of product where one of the main goals of your weekly training is to sell them other things, make sure that your price to point out in your weekly continuity is a lot lower. Okay? You notice that – for a good example of our print newsletter, we have 44 pages every single month we ship out. And there's usually like one or two ads. And it's always for our own products in the magazine, nothing else. Now, the difference between my magazine I can charge, you know, almost $50 for versus a magazine in the store that they can charge $3 for is the one in the store that's got 98% of it are ads. Okay? So if you're going to have a lot of ads through your process, don't charge nearly as much because people will get upset. But, you know, there's a fine line in there, but you can definitely use this process to promote the products as well. You're just teaching a process. In fact, if you guys ordered the use of making money product, I had a CD and they're talking about how selling it in a bad economy can be tough right now and how the easiest way to sell the bad economy is to give away – is to kind of lead with this information and make it a lot lower price point or even free. But then through this teaching of the info product is where you recommend the tools and the resources and those type of things to get people. And that's where you make the money is in the recommendations of the tools and the services. And now we talked a lot about that in that product, but that's kind of the concept there. Okay. Barry says, Russell, I am doing an e-book as a free shipping and handling offer. Do I want to put that on a CD or set a physical book? Great question. Great question. Again, it depends on – a lot of on cost because the printed book is usually going to be $6 to $7 or $8 depending on how big it is, how many you ordered at a time, that kind of stuff. And CDs are a lot cheaper. So it depends on how you want to structure with the cost. And I don't think either one's better or worse. In fact, I ordered someone else's continuity the other day and it was a book on Craigslist and it came on CD and I put the CD in and it opened an e-book. And it's really easy to position. I can just position it, you know, this book is so powerful or so, whatever, that I don't want it getting up there and, you know, I want everyone in the world to give their hands on it. So because of that, we are, you know, are burning it on CD and giving it access that way or something like that. But yeah, either way works. I know a lot of people that have been submitting stuff back to me are doing – having books that are putting them on CDs just because the cost is so much cheaper to be able to send out. And you can still rationalize getting the credit card information, which is what you need to be able to do with microcontinuity. Awesome. Okay, Rebecca is next. She says, for example, Russell, how do you time the beginning of a continuity program? She means how long after the CD is received by the customers, you know, do they get access to the continuity program? Oh, that's awesome. I love that question because there's different ways to do it. If you're doing something shipping, you're dead on. I'm glad you asked that question. If you're doing something digital, you know, on our digital continuity program, we're 100% digital, so like a free trial, you know, $1 trial or 14 days or whatever, we usually make it, excuse me, a very short period of time, like seven days. Because it's completely free. They're getting access immediately. But you're right. When you're shipping something out, you normally have to wait at least long enough for them to get the product before you start billing them. So on the low end, you're looking at 14 days of the hire and maybe 30 days. The closer to 14 days, obviously, the better so you can start refooping some of your advertising costs. Depending on who you're fulfilling is how fast it is, you might want to wait up to even a month to start the billing. Great. Okay, I had a couple of questions pop up here about outsourcing. You know, for example, you know, can you outsource all this stuff, and where's the best place to get it done? Yeah, and if you didn't know my background, I'm a huge outsourcer. I mean, I don't do anything anymore. I'm pretty useless if it really comes down to it. But, yeah, there's a lot of places to outsource. If you're outsourcing programming, the fastest and cheapest place is a site called ScriptLamps.com, and I'll pull it up. So ScriptLamps is where I go for outsourcing programming, because really cheap, it's fast, there's a ton of programmers there. So anything, especially anything a little like, you know, I need a website set up, I need an auto-responder, any of that, I just go to ScriptLamps.com, and that's where I get that kind of stuff done. So here's ScriptLamps. Anything that's more of a more complex program or product or, you know, content, my new favorite service is called odesk.com. And the cool thing about this is actually you can log in and you can watch your workers while they're working on your computer. Because that's what happens, you see, I'm working on a restless project right now, and when they do that, it opens up a window where you can watch them working. So you see if something's actually working. If they're not working, you can fire them or you can yell at them. So it's kind of a nice thing. And again, on odesk, you can find people who write, who do programming, who do graphics, who do pretty much everything across the board, you can find it at odesk. Those are the two that I really recommend right now. And those are the two that I really use personally right now. Great. Okay, Craig says, in regard to the microcontinuity content, does it matter if we do audio or written or video, et cetera, is one better than the other? That's hard to say because everybody consumes content in different ways. Okay, for example, personally, I can't stand watching videos and I can't stand reading by love audio. I know that I'm not the main person. I know that I might not be the majority, I guess, is what I'm trying to say. So a couple ways you can pick which everyone you feel most comfortable with. Like I said, a lot of our videos are videos. But if you want to kind of cover all your bases, what I would do is I would start with a video, strip out the audio and have some transcripts that transcribe the audio. Then you got video, audio, and text. But you never get hit everybody perfectly unless you do something like that. So I would say pick one you're most comfortable with and you're going to get people, you know, you're going to find a group of people who are really attracted the way that you deliver your content and the way your style, and they're going to stick with you. But it's going to be impossible to please everybody unless you do basically three formatted versions. I like video mostly right now just because for me, video is the fastest thing you need to create. And videos is such a big thing that people are more used to consuming content through video online. And so especially with YouTube and all these services, people are just, you know, everyone's so used to video now that it's becoming a bigger platform. So I'd say if I had to choose one, I'd say video is the best just based on them. Great. Okay, Chris is back and he says, Russell, can I set up a forced or semi-forced continuity through PayPal or pay.com, or do I need a shopping cart and merchant account? You can do a forced through PayPal and pay.com very easily. A semi-forced, I'm not sure because the way a semi-forced works is you're putting two products into a cart. You're putting your product that's your free CD and then you're putting a product that is the continuity and somebody can delete the continuity from the cart. So I'm not sure if you can do a semi-forced through PayPal and pay.com. That's probably just some research that I'm not sure. But you can do a forced through PayPal, which is basically your first product is free. You have $7 shipping and you start your trial offer at that point. And if not, one shopping cart works really good for a semi-forced because they give you the ability to create a product and a continuity and tie them together into one cart so somebody can pull one out of the cart if they didn't want it. Awesome. That's a lot of good questions today. Yeah. Okay, Brian says, how much roughly should we expect to pay for someone to strip the audio and try to transcribe it? Stripping the audio is really easy. I mean, it's like a two-minute process and you have any kind of video software. If you don't know how to do that, and I personally don't know how to do it, I have a brother that pops into the video editor, pulls the audio out and doesn't make three seconds he's got it. Imagine someone charging you more than five to ten bucks to do that, especially like a Scriplance or something. These guys are all so desperate for it. He's posted a video up on YouTube today. He's stripped the audio. I'll pay you ten bucks. You're going to get a lot of guys who will jump it out really, really fast. The transcribing part takes a little bit longer. Some of that's done and actually typed through it. Or actually, okay, you've got two options. Option one is when you're recording this stuff, there's a software. Someone just told me just so that there's a brand new version. It's like Dragon something that they're using. They said that it's almost as close as a transcription. It's kind of clean up afterwards, but if you're recording and you're recording into this at the same time, it'll transcribe while you're speaking. So again, I never personally used it. I had a friend that was telling me just on and on about how great the new version is of that Dragon speaking software or whatever. Now, for transcriptionists, they charge, seems like for me, it's usually like $100 or $150 for like an hour-long audio. They do it by the word. It's a couple cents per word. And there's a lot of, I think we use e-scriptionist.com. There's a lot of transcriptionist services out there just typing transcriptionists. And there's a lot of them out there.
